stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-38950 [CWE-22]: Vulnerability allowing remote code execution via spear-phishing attachment
stage.description: The threat actors crafted convincing emails targeting specific employees with relevant access. These emails contained malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability (CVE-2023-38950) to execute arbitrary code when opened.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36033 [CWE-94]: Vulnerability allowing unauthorized modification of Windows registry leading to code execution via PowerShell
stage.description: Upon successful execution of the initial payload, PowerShell scripts were executed to download further payloads and establish command and control communication.

overview.summary: The 2022 Ukraine Electric Power Attack campaign targeted water and wastewater systems using spear-phishing techniques to gain initial access. Once inside, they utilized PowerShell for execution and employed various tactics to maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, and ultimately disrupt operations.
